dental.description: Alabama Medicaid provides limited emergency dental services for Medicare-Medicaid dual enrollees. Most Medicare Advantage plans in Alabama offer some dental benefits.
tips: Compare Medicare Advantage plans during Open Enrollment (Oct 15–Dec 7)
Look for plans with $0 preventive dental
Use dental schools in Birmingham for reduced-cost care

vision.description: Original Medicare does not cover routine eye exams or glasses. Alabama Medicare Advantage plans often include vision benefits.
tips: Enroll in a Medicare Advantage plan with vision
Use in-network providers to reduce costs
Annual eye exam may be covered if diabetic

hearingAids.description: Original Medicare does not cover hearing aids. Some Medicare Advantage plans in Alabama include hearing aid allowances.
tips: Check Medicare Advantage plans offering $500–$2,500 hearing aid allowance
Contact ADRS for vocational assistance
Consider OTC hearing aids (FDA-approved since 2022)

nursingHome.description: Medicare Part A covers up to 100 days in a skilled nursing facility (SNF) per benefit period. After day 20, a daily coinsurance applies. Alabama Medicaid covers long-term custodial care for those who qualify.
programs: Alabama State Health Insurance Assistance Program (SHIP)
nursingHome.avgCost: $250–$320/day for semi-private room; Medicare covers days 1–20 fully
tips: Qualify for SNF: 3-day inpatient hospital stay required
Apply for Alabama Medicaid if assets are limited
Contact SHIP at 1-800-243-5463 for free counseling

data_sources: CMS Medicare Advantage Landscape 2025 (plan counts & premiums)
